description: Create a two-column content slide on foundation models for robotics and embodied AI, with explanatory text on the left and a vertical bar chart on embodied AI research paper growth on the right.

header.kicker: Technology Vision 2025 | AI: A Declaration of Autonomy
header.headline: Foundation Models for Robotics / Embodied AI

items.sub: Embodied AI is AI specifically designed to interact with the real world through a physical body. The technical advances driving the generative AI revolution have sparked new interest in embodied AI, with some researchers believing it is the key to achieving the next leap in AI capability. Various approaches for embodied AI are being tested, including incorporating LLMs and VLMs into robotic systems on top of robotic control policies, as well as creating end-to-end robotics foundation models trained on robotics data.

Google Research has been a leader in this field for years. In 2022, Google’s Gato model showed that robotic inputs and outputs could be tokenized and adapted to a language model. Since then, the company has remained a pioneer, creating a series of progressively stronger robotics transformer (RT) models: RT-1, RT-2, and RT-X. Each improves on the one before, aiming to create generalizable robot policies that can be adapted to a range of robots.

items.text: How does it connect to the trend?
items.sub: Innovations in embodied AI are critical to building robots that have greater contextual understanding of the world, communicate with people using natural language, and plan ways to complete complex actions (even ones they haven’t been specifically trained for) or adapt to changing environments.

right.title:
Research into embodied AI is steadily increasing
The development of foundation models is driving a renewed interest in the concept of embodied AI. Continued research and advances in embodied AI are poised to expand the capabilities of robots significantly.
Number of research papers relating to Embodied AI, 2020–2024*

footer.source: Accenture Research analysis on ArXiv papers; Jan 2020 – Oct 2024. Note: 2024 contains partial data through Oct 2024.
